---
title: "Exploring the Growth Rate of the stringy05 Index"
date: 2026-05-22
categories: [scagnostics, minimum spanning tree, stringy05, asymptotic analysis, R]
description: "A theoretical and empirical exploration of the stringy05 scagnostic index."
license: "All Rights Reserved"
copyright: "Copyright © 2026 Tina Rashid Jafari. All rights reserved."
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
execute:
  echo: true
code-fold: true
warning: FALSE
message: FALSE
---

## Aim

The goal of this blog is to understand the asymptotic behavior of the `stringy05` index, defined as

$$
\text{stringy05} =
\frac{{diameter}(T_n)}{{length}(T_n)}.
$$

Here $T_n$ is the Euclidean minimum spanning tree (MST) built on $n$ random bivariate observations.

The denominator is the total Euclidean length of the MST,

$$
L_n
= {length}(T_n) = \sum_{e\in T_n}|e|.
$$

The numerator is the weighted graph diameter of the MST,

$$
D_n
= {diameter}(T_n)
= \max_{u,v\in T_n} d_{T_n}(u,v),
$$
